title: Karemi Bhante for the Deśāvagāśika Sāmāyika (Deśāvagāśika Sāmāyika Pratyākhyāna)
summary: This is the equanimity vow (sāmāyika) that a Jain layperson takes when observing the Deśāvagāśika — the practice of voluntarily shrinking the boundary of one's daily movement and activity for a fixed time. In the same words used to begin an ordinary sāmāyika, the practitioner renounces sinful activity in mind, speech, and body, and binds the vow specifically to the deśāvagāśika observance. It closes with heartfelt confession and the surrender of the wrong-doing self, so that the period of restraint is spent in calm, harmless self-reflection.
ritualPlacement: This belongs to the appendix observances at the close of the book (book pp.104–108), not to any of the five pratikramaṇas. The Deśāvagāśika is the 10th of the twelve lay vows; this pratyākhyāna is the sāmāyika one takes up while observing it. Its text is the standard Karemi Bhante (the first Āvaśyaka, Sāmāiya, normally anchored by `karemi-bhante` in the Devasi flow on book pp.19–26) reused here with one substitution: where the ordinary sāmāyika reads 'jāva niyamaṁ pajjuvāsāmi', this version reads 'jāva desāvagāsiaṁ pajjuvāsāmi', binding the vow to the deśāvagāśika period. The deśāvagāśika procedure references the Devasi sāmāyika 'take' (pp.19–26) and 'conclude' (pp.41–44) pages by word-substitution; it is an appendix vow, not a pratikramaṇa, so it has no independent ritual machinery of its own.
authorAndSource: Canonical liturgical formula (no single named author). The Karemi Bhante / Sāmāyika sūtra is an ancient Ardhamāgadhī Āvaśyaka text shared across the Śvetāmbara tradition; this Deśāvagāśika adaptation is printed in the Anchalgachchha (Vidhipakṣa) edition *Pañca Pratikramaṇa Sūtra – Vidhi Sahit*, the book's appendix on the Deśāvagāśika vow (book pp.104–108).
structureArc: A single verse (v.1) that moves through five compact moments: (a) the address and undertaking — 'karemi bhante! sāmāiyaṁ' (I undertake the sāmāyika, O Venerable one); (b) the renunciation — 'sāvajjaṁ jogaṁ paccakkhāmi' (I renounce sinful activity), bounded by 'jāva desāvagāsiaṁ pajjuvāsāmi' (for as long as I attend upon the deśāvagāśika); (c) the scope formula — 'duvihaṁ tivihaṇaṁ. maṇeṇaṁ, vāyāe, kāeṇaṁ na karemi na kāravemi' (two-fold by three means: by mind, speech, and body I will neither do nor cause to be done); (d) the confession — 'tassa bhante! paḍikkamāmi, nindāmi, garihāmi' (for that I repent, censure, and reproach myself); (e) the surrender — 'appāṇaṁ vosirāmi' (I cast off the [wrong-doing] self).
keyThemes: the tenth lay vow: limiting one's spatial and activity boundary
the two-fold/three-fold scope of a vow
fourfold self-correction: repentance, censure, reproach, surrender

glossary.definition: The vow of equanimity — a fixed period (classically ~48 minutes) of mental calm and detachment, free of sinful activity; the first of the six Āvaśyakas.
The 10th lay vow: voluntarily narrowing, for a set time, the boundaries of one's movement and activity already limited by the 6th vow (dig-vrata), thereby reducing the scope for harm.
A formal renunciation or vow taken for a coming period; the sixth Āvaśyaka. Here it frames the sāmāyika undertaken for the deśāvagāśika.
Sinful or blameworthy activity — any act of mind, speech, or body that causes harm; what the practitioner renounces for the vow's duration.
'I renounce / I abstain' — the verb of renunciation that enacts the vow.
'For as long as I attend upon …' — the clause fixing the vow's duration; in the ordinary sāmāyika the object is 'niyama', here it is the 'deśāvagāśika' observance.
'Two-fold by three means' — the scope formula: abstaining from both doing and causing to be done (two-fold), through mind, speech, and body (three means).
'I turn back / I repent' — withdrawal from transgression, the root act of pratikramaṇa.
'I censure (myself)' — inward self-criticism of one's faults.
'I reproach (myself before the guru)' — outward acknowledgement of faults in the teacher's presence.
'I cast off the self' — renouncing the wrong-doing self and its attachments for the period of the vow.
'O Venerable one' — respectful address to the guru/Tīrthaṅkara before whom the vow is declared.

howToRead: A newcomer can read this as a single short pledge — 'I take up equanimity, give up harmful activity for this period, and own up to my faults.' Advanced practitioners should note it is the familiar Karemi Bhante reused verbatim except for the 'jāva desāvagāsiaṁ' substitution, so its commentary need only foreground what the deśāvagāśika setting adds, not re-explain the sāmāyika formula itself.

sources.title: Pañca Pratikramaṇa Sūtra – Vidhi Sahit (Anchalgachchha/Vidhipakṣa ed.)
sources.consultedFor: Verbatim sūtra text, appendix placement of the Deśāvagāśika vow (book pp.104–108), and the word-substitution rule.

sources.title: Tattvārtha Sūtra (Umāsvāti), ch.7 on the śrāvaka vratas
sources.consultedFor: Standard enumeration of the twelve lay vows and the place of deśāvakāśika as the 10th vow.

notes: Spelling follows the book's own orthography (e.g. પચ્ચખ્ખામિ / દેસાવગાસિઅં) and is not normalized. The transliteration of the bound clause assumes the deśāvagāśika substitution replaces the ordinary 'jāva niyamaṁ'; the surrounding Karemi Bhante wording is otherwise identical to the standard sāmāyika sūtra. Exact mātrās/anusvāras should be taken from the book as golden source. The source book has no public stable URL; its 'url' is left empty rather than fabricated.
